title: CropGraph Pest and Disease Associations
description:
The fourth edge layer of the CropGraph, directed associations between crops and the pests or diseases that affect them. Each entry has a crop slug (validated against crop-calendar.json at load time), a pest or disease slug (kebab-case, in its own namespace), a `type` flag distinguishing pest from disease, a severity grade, diagnostic symptoms text, an array of organic management options, prevention text, an array of regions where the pest/disease is significant, and a citation. The same pest can appear against many crops (cabbage worm is on cabbage, broccoli, kale, collards, etc.); per-entry symptoms and management are crop-specific even when the underlying agent is shared.

Evidence grounding: Cornell Cooperative Extension, UC IPM Online, UF/IFAS Extension, Penn State Extension, OSU Extension, WSU Extension, Texas A&M AgriLife Extension, Rodale Encyclopedia of Organic Gardening, USDA-ARS pest profiles. Management is intentionally restricted to OMRI-listed materials, biocontrols, and physical/cultural practices; conventional synthetic pesticides are out of scope for this fixture.

Severity grades are calibrated for the gardener: `severe` warrants scouting twice weekly and warrants resistant cultivars or long rotations; `high` causes significant yield loss; `moderate` is cosmetic or partial; `low` is tolerable in most beds and sometimes a deliberate trade-off (e.g. parsleyworm on dill).

version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes only.

source.description: Provenance / curation block for the whole fixture.

license.description: License under which the data is redistributed (e.g. CC-BY-4.0).

lastUpdated.description: ISO date of the last hand-curation pass.

entries.description: Crop-to-pest/disease association edges. Order is not significant; helpers index by crop and pest slug at load time.

crop.description: Subject crop slug. Must exist in crop-calendar.json.

pest.description: Kebab-case identifier for the pest or disease. Distinct namespace from crop slugs. Same `pest` slug appears against many crops when the agent is shared.

type.description: Whether the agent is an animal (`pest`: insects, mites, mollusks, nematodes, vertebrates) or a pathogen (`disease`: fungi, oomycetes, bacteria, viruses, plus physiological disorders like blossom end rot).

severity.description: Calibrated for the gardener. `severe` warrants resistant cultivars and long rotations; `high` causes significant yield loss; `moderate` is partial / cosmetic; `low` is tolerable.

symptoms.description: Diagnostic prose for the gardener with a hand lens. Concrete and recognition-focused, not encyclopedic.

organicManagement.description: Ordered list of organic management options. OMRI-listed materials, biocontrols, and physical/cultural practices only. Conventional synthetics are out of scope.

prevention.description: Cultural and pre-planting practices that reduce future pressure.

regions.description: Where the pest/disease is significant. `all` for ubiquitous problems; otherwise informal region labels (`southern-us`, `pnw`, `arid`, `eastern-us`).

source.description: Citation, extension service, or research publication. Multiple sources separated by semicolons.
